compositional_truth_check.findings: P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of visible foliage. P3 (Black Pot) is present, containing the white rabbit anchor and soil debris. P2 and P4 are not clearly distinguishable as separate entities, suggesting a potential consolidation or loss.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil surface; no signs of succulent growth.
plant_audit.p2_p4: No distinct plant structures identified; soil appears dormant.
plant_audit.p3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white granular material (likely perlite or mineral deposits) on the surface of P3.
biome_observations.anomalies: Introduction of a white spoon/tool in T-3 and a cylindrical object in T-2/CURRENT.

temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: Introduction of a metallic spoon in the yellow pot area.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Spoon removed; introduction of a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or marker) near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_t1: Significant lighting drop; visual data obscured.
temporal_deltas.t1_to_current: Return of lighting; no recovery of plant life observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total systemic failure. No active photosynthetic tissue is visible in any of the monitored containers. The environment is effectively a 'dead' soil system.
anomalies: Introduction of a cylindrical sensor/marker (T-2 to CURRENT)
Persistent absence of plant matter despite the presence of soil and anchors.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete lack of viable plant life across all registered pots. The 'String of Nickels', 'Mexican Mint', 'Pothos', and 'Silver Guest' are not present in their respective containers. The environment appears to be undergoing a period of maintenance or monitoring, evidenced by the temporary placement of tools and sensors, but the biological components have suffered a total systemic loss.
